summary: All 7 D-wrong rows share the SAME mechanism: convention mismatch (Class A) at the prior 10-K. The prior periodic is a Q4 10-K with FYE in Jan/Feb. Both XBRL DocumentFiscalYearFocus and period_to_fiscal() use year_of_end (e.g., period 2024-02-03 → FY=2024), so D's FY-disagreement gate finds no disagreement and admits the prior. But the company's EX-99.1 labels that same 10-K with year_of_start (e.g., 'fiscal year 2023'). D then advances Q4→Q1 with FY+1 from the XBRL year, producing FY off-by-one (e.g., FY=2025 Q1 instead of FY=2024 Q1). The current Q1 10-Q's companion XBRL agrees with SEC truth, so a Q1-companion-XBRL rule would recover all 7 — but none of the 6 Goal 6f candidates target the Q4→Q1 convention skew at the prior 10-K, so recovered_by_any_goal6f_cand is empty for every wrong row.

prior_periodic.ex99_label: 'Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2023 Results' / 'fourth quarter and fiscal year 2023 results' (tm247967d1_ex99-1.htm)

per_row.evidence: Prior 10-K (period 2024-02-03) has XBRL DocumentFiscalYearFocus=2024 (year_of_end), period_to_fiscal()=(2024, Q4) — both agree, so rule G's FY-disagreement gate does not fire. But the same 10-K's EX-99.1 explicitly labels it 'fourth quarter and fiscal year 2023' (year_of_start convention used by Kroger in its press release). D advances FY=2024 Q4 → FY=2025 Q1; truth is FY=2024 Q1.

prior_periodic.ex99_label: 'Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2024 Results' / 'fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024' (tm258221d1_ex99-1.htm)

per_row.evidence: Prior 10-K (period 2025-02-01) has XBRL FY=2025 (year_of_end). period_to_fiscal()=(2025, Q4) agrees → no fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2025 Q4 → FY=2026 Q1; truth is FY=2025 Q1.

Prior 10-K (period 2024-02-03) has XBRL FY=2024 (year_of_end). period_to_fiscal()=(2024, Q4) agrees → no fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2023' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2024 Q4 → FY=2025 Q1; truth is FY=2024 Q1.

Prior 10-K (period 2025-02-01) has XBRL FY=2025 (year_of_end). period_to_fiscal()=(2025, Q4) agrees → no fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2024' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2025 Q4 → FY=2026 Q1; truth is FY=2025 Q1.

prior_periodic.ex99_label: 'Dave & Buster's Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year End 2024 Financial Results' / 'fourth quarter and fiscal year ended February 4, 2025' / 'fiscal 2024' (play-2024q4er8kxexx991.htm)

per_row.evidence: Prior 10-K (period 2025-02-04) has XBRL FY=2025 (year_of_end). period_to_fiscal()=(2025, Q4) agrees → no fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year End 2024' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2025 Q4 → FY=2026 Q1; truth is FY=2025 Q1. Source string includes _effective_fye_from_prior_10k because PLAY's fye_month was inferred from this prior 10-K, but the underlying class is the same convention mismatch.

prior_periodic.ex99_label: 'FOURTH QUARTER AND FISCAL YEAR 2022 FINANCIAL RESULTS' (rh-20230329xex99d2.htm)

per_row.evidence: Prior 10-K (period 2023-01-28) has XBRL FY=2023 (year_of_end of fiscal year ending Jan 2023). period_to_fiscal(2023-01-28, fye=1, 10-K) returns (2023, Q4) — agrees with XBRL. No fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'FOURTH QUARTER AND FISCAL YEAR 2022' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2023 Q4 → FY=2024 Q1; truth is FY=2023 Q1.

Prior 10-K (period 2024-02-03) has XBRL FY=2024 (year_of_end). period_to_fiscal()=(2024, Q4) agrees → no fail-closed. EX-99.1 labels this same 10-K 'FOURTH QUARTER AND FISCAL YEAR 2023' (year_of_start). D advances FY=2024 Q4 → FY=2025 Q1; truth is FY=2024 Q1.

caveats: All 7 wrong-fires occur at the FY-boundary Q4→Q1 hop. The prior 10-K is the structurally vulnerable point because for FYE-Jan/Feb companies, period_to_fiscal() and XBRL DocumentFiscalYearFocus BOTH use year_of_end (e.g., period 2024-02-03 → FY=2024), so they agree and the FY-disagreement guard never fires; the company's EX-99 still uses year_of_start ('fiscal year 2023').

This is structurally identical to BOX/GIII's 'mixed convention' issue, except KR/OLLI/PLAY/RH never enter the Q4 10-K through Tier-A audit (10-K Q4 rows fail closed via different codepaths). It's the SUBSEQUENT Q1 8-K that gets wrong-fired because D consults the convention-skewed Q4 10-K as its prior.

All 7 wrong rows have empty recovered_by_any_goal6f_cand — the 6 Goal 6f candidates do not address Q4-prior convention skew because the FY-agreement gate (XBRL vs period_to_fiscal) is satisfied at the prior, so any candidate gated on FY-disagreement at the prior cannot fire.

A Q1-companion-XBRL-trust rule (i.e., when the 8-K is followed within hours by a 10-Q whose XBRL fully specifies FY/Q, prefer that over Q4-prior projection) would recover all 7. Each row has companion_periodic_filed within 0–8 days of filed_8k and companion_xbrl_fy/q matching SEC truth.

The companion 10-Qs for these rows have period_day > 5 (KR 25, OLLI 4 [<=5], PLAY 6, RH 4 [<=5]), so the period_to_fiscal day-shift heuristic does not affect the Q1 companion's interpretation — but the prior 10-K hits day_of_period <= 5 for KR/OLLI/RH (Feb 3, Feb 4, Feb 1) and day=4 for PLAY (Feb 4 → adjusted to January), so the day-shift heuristic actively contributes to picking year_of_end at the prior.
